data.title: Google faces EU antitrust complaint over AI Overviews as independent publishers seek action - Reuters
data.description:
Alphabet's (NASDAQ:GOOGL) (NASDAQ:GOOG) Google has reportedly been hit by an EU antitrust complaint over its AI Overviews from a group of independent publishers, which has also asked for an interim measure to prevent allegedly irreparable harm to them.

Google's AI Overviews are AI-generated summaries that appear above traditional hyperlinks to relevant webpages and are shown to users in more than 100 countries. It began adding advertisements to AI Overviews last May.

The tech giant is making its most ambitious move yet by integrating AI into its search platform, but the shift has raised alarms among content providers, particularly publishers.

In a document dated June 30, seen by Reuters, the Independent Publishers Alliance filed a formal complaint with the European Commission, accusing Google of exploiting its dominance in online search, harming independent media.

"Google's core search engine service is misusing web content for Google's AI Overviews in Google Search, which have caused, and continue to cause, significant harm to publishers, including news publishers in the form of traffic, readership and revenue loss,"  the document said.

The complaints echoed a U.S. lawsuit by a U.S. edtech company, which said Google's AI Overviews is eroding demand for original content and undermining publishers' ability to compete that has resulting in a drop in visitors and subscribers.

data.title: ORIX USA to acquire a majority stake in Hilco Globa
Humana's CenterWell to buy The Villages Health amid chapter 11 bankruptcy
data.description:
• The Villages Health—a major healthcare provider in Florida—announced a strategic restructuring that includes a voluntary Chapter 11 bankruptcy filing and an agreement to be acquired by CenterWell, a subsidiary of Humana (NYSE:HUM).
  • The Villages Health initiated bankruptcy after discovering significant medicare billing discrepancies in Fall 2024, which could lead to hundreds of millions in overpayments and penalties.
  • CenterWell has entered a "stalking horse" asset purchase agreement to acquire TVH’s eight primary care centers and two specialty care centers as a going concern.
  • The sale is subject to court approval and an auction process, where other parties may submit competing bids.
  • The acquisition is positioned as a move to preserve and enhance patient care, with both organizations assuring the community of a seamless transition.

data.title: Nvidia's stock gains put it nearly on track to become world's most valuable company in history
data.description:
On Thursday, Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) was briefly on track to become the most valuable company in history after its market capitalization hit about $3.92T.

Shares of the AI chipmaker jumped up to 2.4% to $160.98 on Thursday, giving it a higher market value than that of Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) when the iPhone maker touched a closing value of $3.915T on Dec. 26, 2024.

The shares traded in the range of $157.77 to $160.98 on Thursday. The market cap was $3.84T.

Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) is currently the second-most valuable company on Wall Street, with a market cap of about $3.65T, followed by Apple with a market value of nearly 3.17T.

Nvidia's chips have been in high demand by companies building AI models as they need faster computing capacity.

Nvidia is now worth more than the combined value of the Canadian and Mexican stock markets, Reuters reported, citing LSEG data. The Santa Clara, Calif.-based company also exceeds the total value of all publicly listed companies in the U.K.

data.title: Alstom bags €2billion deal from New York Metropolitan Transportation Authority
Trump to start sending tariff letters on Friday, gives 10%-70% range
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump said that his administration will start sending letters to trading partners on Friday, July 4, specifying the tariff rates that they will have to pay. He added that because it is difficult to make deals with over 170 countries, Washington will now send letters to 10 countries at a time.

Trump told reporters, before leaving for Iowa on Thursday, that about "10 or 12" letters will be sent first, with more to follow "over the next few days." The proposed tariff rates would "range in value from maybe 60 or 70% tariffs to 10 and 20% tariffs." 

“We have more than 170 countries, and how many deals can you make?” Trump said. “They’re very much more complicated.” “I think by the ninth they’ll be fully covered,” he said, referencing the end of the tariff pause.

These tariffs will take effect on August 1, 2025, unless countries reach separate trade agreements with the U.S. before a July 9 deadline.

“We have a couple of other deals, but my inclination is to send a letter out and say what tariffs they are going to be paying.” This suggests limited room for new agreements as July 9 approaches.

If the highest rates are applied, they would surpass the initially proposed tariffs in April during his "Liberation Day" statement, which ranged from 10% to a maximum of 50%. Trump has long threatened to impose higher rates on those who are unable to negotiate agreements before the deadline. 

So far, the administration has announced agreements with Vietnam and the UK and reached a truce with China that resulted in the lowering of reciprocal tariffs between the two biggest economies in the world.

Despite earlier market voices suggesting that the U.S. might consider an extension, Trump ruled that out, stating he is “not considering” moving the deadline. It looks like the negotiations window is rapidly closing.

ETFs to track the broader U.S. markets: (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: ECB ramps up efforts to assess climate, nature risks when setting monetary policy
data.description:
An unprecedented heatwave has hit parts of Europe, with Spain, England, France, and Italy seeing record temperatures for this time of the year. Meanwhile, Germany, Greece, Turkey have been battling wildfires.

"As a result of human-induced climate change, extreme heat is becoming more frequent and intense," the UN's World Meteorological Organization noted, adding that more than two thirds of the most severe heatwaves in Europe since 1950 have occurred since 2000.

In this backdrop, the European Central Bank this week committed to fully take into account the implications of climate change and nature degradation for monetary policy.

In an interview with Bloomberg, European Central Bank official Frank Elderson warned of the impact of heatwaves on the region's economy.

"We have progressed in understanding that accounting for the climate and nature crises is relevant," he said. "If you think about the exceptionally hot summer of 2022, food-price inflation was up between 0.4 and 0.9 percentage points."

Elderson added that the ECB is still studying the impacts of events like the summer of 2022 on inflation and GDP to get a sense of what's ahead.

data.title: UMC reports 7% sales growth in June 2025
data.description:
• United Microelectronics Corporation (NYSE:UMC) announced net sales for June 2025, reporting a notable increase compared to the previous year.
  • For the month of June 2025, UMC's revenues reached 18,823,070, marking a 7.26% increase Y/Y from 17,548,413 in June 2024.
  • This positive momentum also extended to the first half of the year, with cumulative revenues for January through June 2025 totaling 116,616,614. This represents a 4.65% rise compared to 111,431,389 recorded in the same period of 2024.
  • UMC's stock price has seen significant gains YTD, rising nearly 21%.
  • However, both Seeking Alpha's quant rating and Wall Street analysts currently assign the stock a Hold rating.

data.title: European markets lose steam ahead of July 9 deadline
data.description:
London (UKX) -0.24% to 8,801.

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.53% to 23,808, as nervousness surrounding global trade unsettled investors. Factory orders in Germany dropped by 1.4% M/M in May.

France (CAC:IND) -0.75% to 7,697. Industrial production in France decreased by 0.5% M/M in May.

The annual inflation rate in the Czech Republic rose to 2.9% in June.

Industrial production in Hungary dropped by 2.6% Y/Y in May. 

The Swiss unemployment rate held steady at a non-seasonally adjusted 2.7% in June.

Industrial production in Spain rose 1.7% Y/Y in May.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) dropped 0.54% to 540.87, marking a loss of 1.88% during the last one month. Market attention shifted back to global trade tensions ahead of the July 9 deadline. U.S. President Trump said that Washington will send formal notifications to trading partners on Friday, detailing the tariff rates they will face on exports to the U.S.

The EU-U.S. trade negotiations wrapped up without a breakthrough. EU Trade Commissioner Maros Sefcovic said that while talks would continue. The EU is pushing for an 'agreement in principle' ahead of July 9 but has acknowledged that a comprehensive deal is unlikely to be reached by the deadline

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 6 basis points to 4.35%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down 4 basis points to 2.59%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 2 basis points to 4.55%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: McEwen Mining announces name change to McEwen Inc.
data.description:
• McEwen Mining (NYSE:MUX) said that it will officially change its name to McEwen Inc., effective July 7, 2025.
  • The company will begin trading on the NYSE under its new name, McEwen Inc., on July 7, 2025, and on the TSX shortly thereafter. There will be no change in the company’s CUSIP or stock symbol.

  • This rebranding reflects the company’s evolving strategy and broader vision for the future.
